title: Lab 1 Notes
text:
Develop basic R programming skills (e.g., creating vectors, performing operations, installing and using packages) ￼
Apply fundamental statistical functions such as mean, median, minimum, maximum, and standard deviation ￼

title: Lab 1 Notes
text:
Develop basic R programming skills (e.g., creating vectors, performing operations, installing and using packages) ￼
Apply fundamental statistical functions such as mean, median, minimum, maximum, and standard deviation ￼

title: Lab 1 Notes
text:
Notes
We will use the following notes to cover material for Lab 1

Fundamentals of R
Stats 10 Lab 1 Handout

title: Lab 1 Notes
text:
Questions

Create a code chunk below this question and print the statement: “My name is (your name) and my UID is (your UID). I am looking forward to Stats 10!”
Create a vector named titles and fill it with at least 3 book titles. Print the vector titles.
Run the code chunk below using titles. Read the error message and explain why an error occurs.


sum(titles)


Create a vector named vector_log and make it hold the values TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE. Try multiplying the values by 2 and using the mean() function. Explain your results and how R treats logical values.
You are given a vector containing the scores of 50 students on a recent exam. Find the minimum, maximum, mean, and standard deviation of the scores. Print each value (for example, print “Mean: ___”).


# You do not have to change this code
set.seed(123456789)
scores <- sample(0:100, 50, replace = TRUE)
print(scores)

 [1]  65  15  43  44  85  38  11  31   8  43   9  25  20  22  37  57  14  43  69
[20]  67  12  45   8  31  65 100  19  92   6  44  76  51  48  71   0  72  78  66
[39]  79  44  14  82  12  84  48  42  22  45  45  38



Using the scores vector, find the mean value without using the mean() function. (Hint: The mean is calculated by finding the sum of all values and dividing by the total number of values.)


Explain whether there is a difference between the means found in #5 and #6 and why it does or does not exist.


Knit the file into a PDF and name it “yourname_yourUID Stats 10 Lab 1”. Submit the file into Canvas.

title: Welcome To STATS140XP
text:
Instructor: Jose Toledo Luna
Office Hours: Wednesday 1:00pm - 1:50pm 




Use the following google form to submit your personal website
Google Form
You must use your ucla gmail account to submit the form.
The minimum requirements for your webiste should include

About page
Profile picutre
Downloable CV/Resume PDF
Projects/Research Tab: Some of your past work to showcase future employers, this can be presentations, blogs, reports etc…
Links to socials such as linkedin, github, email, etc…

All other customizations are welcome. This is your portfolio make it as simple or complex as youd like.
Deadline: December 07, 2025 @ 11:59pm
Late submissions will be penalized.
